name: Pennsylvania State Grant
description: Pennsylvania's primary state grant program for residents attending eligible Pennsylvania colleges. One of the largest state grant programs in the country with significant funding.
eligibility: Pennsylvania resident, U.S. citizen or eligible non-citizen, enrolled at least half-time in an eligible PA degree program, demonstrating financial need based on PA State Grant means test, meeting SAP requirements, and maintaining enrollment in approved program.
award_amount: Varies from $500 to $4,738 per year based on need, enrollment intensity, and cost of attendance. Full-time students from families with countable income up to approximately $103,000 may qualify. Part-time awards are prorated.
application_process: File FAFSA by May 1 priority deadline. PA residents who attend PA colleges are automatically considered. Students at some out-of-state colleges may need to complete the Direct State Grant form through PHEAA.
timeline: May 1 priority deadline. Renewal students have until August 1. Award notifications typically sent April-June. Must maintain SAP (2.0 GPA and completion of 67% of attempted credits).
advisor_notes: PA State Grant has specific residency requirements — students must have been PA residents for 12 months before the first day of classes. The program has generous income limits compared to many states. Non-traditional students and those attending part-time have specific rules. Appeal process exists for students who lose eligibility due to SAP.
stacking_notes: PA State Grant stacks with Pell Grant. Federal grants and state grants typically count toward meeting need. The self-help expectation (loans + work-study) is typically $3,000 for full-time students. If total gift aid (grants + scholarships) exceeds need, PA State Grant may be reduced.
